title: Lasagne Soup
summary: An incredibly easy soup, bursting with flavour. It’s the perfect way to use up the last few lasagne sheets that for some reason never make it into an actual lasagne.

method.description: Peel the garlic and trim the chillies. Put all the base ingredients into a blender and blend until smooth.

Place a saucepan over a medium heat, then add a drizzle of olive oil. Tip in the pepper mixture and cook for 3-4 mins, stirring until thicker. Pour in the stock and let it come to a boil, then turn the heat down to medium and simmer the soup for 10-15 mins.
Meanwhile, discard the stems of the cavolo nero and tear the leaves into small bite-sized chunks. Break the lasagne sheets into halves.
Squeeze the sausagemeat from the casings. Add a drizzle of olive oil to a new pan and, once hot, cook the sausagemeat (break it up using a spatula) for 5-8 mins until crispy.

Add the lasagne sheets to the soup one by one, then cook for 3 mins, stirring occasionally to separate them from each other. Stir through the cavolo nero and cook for another 3-5 mins until both the pasta and cavolo nero are tender.
Share the soup between bowls, then top with yoghurt and crispy sausagemeat.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
